us.headline: Tariff stacks and origin rules — not mill prices — drive your landed cost.

regulation.b: China-origin goods carry additive Section 301 duties (many lists 25%; consumer List 4A 7.5%). Origin and HTS classification materially change landed cost; confirm each item's status before costing.
Duties compose additively (MFN + Section 301 + any Section 232 on steel/aluminium). Confirm each product's HTS code before costing.

us.supply: Asia–US (transpacific) spot rates are surging on early peak-season demand, pre-tariff frontloading and 2026 FIFA World Cup cargo (Drewry's composite World Container Index rose 9% to US$4,530/FEU on 02 July 2026, with Shanghai to Los Angeles up 10% to $6,349/FEU and Shanghai to New York up 11% to $7,902/FEU) — booking space early matters more than chasing a dip. Brent crude surged to $76.01 per barrel on July 8, 2026, due to escalating tensions and attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with the US revoking Iranian oil sales authorizations. This keeps resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile.
us.view: US landed cost is dominated by tariffs and origin, not spot material prices. Birdland's two-country production (Taiwan + China) lets you shift origin to manage tariff exposure, and we prepare clear origin documentation. With transpacific freight now firming into peak season, the smart move is to lock capacity and contract rates early — our pricing reflects secured capacity and compliant origin, not a single day's index.

ca.headline: ‘Country of melt’ now decides whether China steel surtaxes hit you.

regulation.b: Since 31 Jul 2025 Canada applies a 25% surtax on steel melted & poured (and aluminium cast) in China, on top of normal duties. From 26 Dec 2025 a 25% tariff also covers listed steel-derivative products — some with only partial steel content.

Several temporary remissions narrowed from 1 Jul 2026 — now in effect — including the end of relief for aluminium, public-health/safety goods, and motor-vehicle and aircraft inputs, though a Steel Derivative Goods Surtax Remission Order (Feb 2026; CBSA guidance Mar 2026) keeps category-based relief for a defined list. Confirm classification, country of melt and remission eligibility before shipping.

ca.supply: Landed steel cost into Canada is shaped more by origin-of-melt surtaxes than by mill prices. Brent crude surged to $76.01 per barrel on July 8, 2026, due to escalating tensions and attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, with the US revoking Iranian oil sales authorizations. This keeps resin and energy-linked input costs highly volatile.
ca.view: Birdland can source Taiwan-melted steel content with clear traceability, helping you stay outside the China-melt surtax, and we document origin end-to-end. The driver here is provenance and paperwork, not spot steel — our quotes reflect verified origin and stable supply.

summary.changed: Brent crude surged due to Strait of Hormuz escalation, increasing energy-linked input costs.
summary.action: Verify product classification and country of melt, and consider locking fuel-linked inputs.

Per-unit and order-total outputs translate market moves into real purchase-order impact.

p-tariff.simple: A traffic-light board for import taxes — red means a new toll just appeared on your route.
expert: It tracks recent and pending duty changes by market, including US Section 301 proposals and Canada's steel surtax.
Country of origin and country-of-melt increasingly decide whether a surtax applies.
Pair it with the landed-cost calculator to quantify each change before it hits a costing.

p-war.simple: A storm-tracker for your supply chain — it shows which conflicts could blow shipments off course.
expert: Each event is scored 0–100 for pressure on oil, freight, transit time and insurance.
Strait-of-Hormuz and Red Sea risks feed fuel and freight volatility most directly.
Birdland's two-country production buffers single-point shocks, keeping supply moving if one route is hit.

p-mkt.simple: Like reading two top weather stations (BNP Paribas & Citi), then telling you what to pack for your market.
expert: Cross-referencing publicly reported BNP Paribas and Citi research: Citi sees Brent crude stabilizing around ~US$70-75/bbl into late 2026, with a gradual upward trend, impacting fuel- and freight-linked costs.
BNP Paribas flags tariff-reconfiguration risk (the US tariff court ruling, USMCA renegotiation) and China–EU trade tensions as key watch-items, alongside ongoing Red Sea disruptions.

Birdland's takeaway for your sourcing: treat current energy stability as a planning cushion, not a reason to chase spot — lock supply and keep origin flexible while tariff rules and geopolitical risks are in flux.

c-desk.simple: This is the desk's one-line 'what it all means' — like a teacher summing up the whole chapter in a sentence.
expert: It distils regulation, tariffs and freight into the few moves that actually change your landed cost.
Read it first, then drill into the panels below for the detail behind each point.
AI-assisted and refreshed regularly — framed as Birdland's view, not advice.

c-watch.simple: A scoreboard for your raw materials — green up, red down, like a stock ticker for your cost inputs.
expert: Each row is an input (oil, steel, resin, freight, tariff pressure) with its latest level, change and trend.
Hover a row for what that move means for your landed cost.
Use it as the at-a-glance pulse before reading the deeper panels.

c-freight.simple: These are the speedometers of shipping — bulk (BDI) and container (FEU) show how fast freight costs move.
expert: FRED's deep-sea PPI and Cass index are official, free monthly readings of freight cost and volume.
BDI signals bulk/dry demand; container FEU rates drive your boxed-goods landed cost.
Monthly series lag 1–2 months — use the official source links for the latest spot figures.

c-curve.simple: Like a tide chart — it tells you if prices are heading up (lock now) or down (wait).
expert: A rising curve (contango) means forward prices are higher, so locking now can pay off.
A falling curve (backwardation) means the market expects easing — less urgency.
Steel, oil, resin and freight each give their own procurement-timing signal.

c-cost.simple: Shows what really fills your cost pie — raw material is just one slice, not the whole pie.
expert: Conversion, freight, duty and service dominate landed cost, so a dip in one index moves your price far less than the headline.
That's why Birdland's quotes reflect secured materials and reliable supply, not yesterday's spot price.
Hover a segment to see each component's share.

c-duty.simple: A toll calculator for imports — pick the country and the origin, and it shows the import tax on your route.
expert: Switching origin (Taiwan vs China) shows how much tariff stacking you avoid.
Figures are illustrative for garden hand-tools — confirm your HS code with your broker.
Pair it with the cost breakdown to see duty's real weight.

BNP Paribas flags tariff-reconfiguration risk (the US tariff court ruling, USMCA renegotiation) and China–EU trade tensions as key watch-items, alongside ongoing Red Sea disruptions.

Birdland's takeaway for your sourcing: treat current energy stability as a planning cushion, not a reason to chase spot — lock supply and keep origin flexible while tariff rules and geopolitical risks are in flux.

usdtwd_view.text: Near-term USD/TWD leans to mild TWD depreciation: a still-patient Fed and lingering US–China trade friction support the dollar, while Taiwan's central bank maintains a steady stance. Watch a 31.8–32.2 range over the next 1–4 weeks; exporters may phase in forward cover rather than wait.

advice.zh: 【Birdland 供應鏈週報】本週亞洲—歐洲與亞洲—美西海運運價均大幅走升,進入旺季高峰;鋼價因CBAM碳成本與需求支撐偏強,木材與塑膠粒則持續走軟。建議:歐洲線與美西線均應提前訂艙鎖運費,並分批提前鎖定鋼質料件。法規面 EUDR(2026/12/30)木柄文件請務必提早備齊。需要我們協助規劃備料節奏與文件,隨時聯繫。
advice.en: [Birdland Supply-Chain Brief] Asia–Europe and transpacific ocean freight rates are both surging into peak season; steel stays firm due to CBAM carbon costs and demand, while lumber and resin continue to soften. We suggest booking all peak-season ocean space early to lock freight and phasing in steel-input cover. On compliance, prepare EUDR (30 Dec 2026) timber documentation early and thoroughly. Contact us to plan your buying cadence and paperwork.

materials_bot.simple: 原料像做菜的食材——鋼和包材在漲、木材和塑膠在跌,整體成本『一半漲一半跌』,不會單看一項就決定報價。
expert: 對台灣出口:鋼與電鍍/包材偏漲,鋼質手工具到岸成本壓力升,建議分批鎖鋼料、優化包材規格。
對中國出口:木材與樹膠走軟有利木柄/握把成本,但 EUDR(木材)合規文件成關鍵門檻。
綜合:成本結構分散,維持台灣+中國雙基地調度,用『鎖料+文件齊備』把波動轉成穩定供應的賣點。

shipping_bot.simple: 海運成本像搭車的車資——歐洲線塞車漲價、美西順暢降價;港口和拖車的『小費』也在變。
expert: 對台灣/中國出口:歐洲線運價與旺季附加偏漲,建議提前訂艙、鎖長約;美西回軟可保留彈性。
內陸拖運與海關倉儲/查驗成本上升,出貨節奏與文件齊備能直接省下滯港與查驗成本。
綜合:把運費波動納入報價(BAF/PSS 先估),用提前佈局把不確定變可預期,是對客戶最有價值的服務。

reg_bot.simple: 法規像考試規則一直在改——歐美越來越看『木材哪來的、鋼的碳排多少、有沒有強迫勞動』,文件不齊就卡關。
expert: 對台灣出口:CBAM(鋼)、EUDR(木)文件成歐洲市場門檻;台灣熔煉鋼還能規避加拿大附加稅,是優勢。
對中國出口:原產地查驗趨嚴 + 美 UFLPA,『中國製』暴露於關稅與審查,雙基地調度與產地文件是關鍵。
綜合:把『合規文件齊備』做成標準服務,等於替客戶清掉通關風險,是最強的差異化。
